rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft can reasonably include sharpening the contribution claim and framing of verbal-confidence elicitation.
rationale.R1.2: The delegation expects checking whether the argument from setup to results to interpretation is coherent.
rationale.R1.3: Because the draft makes numerical claims from tables and benchmarks, improvement centrally includes aligning claims with the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.5: The excerpt has section-level narrative issues, so improving it includes reorganizing the flow of methods, results, related work, and ablation material.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence- and paragraph-level refinement is load-bearing because 'Improve this draft' directly asks for better prose quality.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of improvement must preserve uncertainty about what is not shown in the artifact.
rationale.RX.2: The improvement responsibility includes preventing stronger claims than the reported evidence supports.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within draft-improvement responsibilities rather than executing unrelated research or evaluation work.
rationale.RX.4: The projection must account for avoiding misleading scientific communication or downstream misuse of unsupported claims.
rationale.RX.5: Changes or judgments would need to remain traceable to the provided draft and its stated tables, benchmarks, and citations.
